content:
The Python features I once ignored are now the ones I reach for first in almost every automation project.

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Juairia Islam Shefa on Unsplashread here.
For the longest time, I thought becoming better at Python meant learning more frameworks.
Whenever a new library became popular, I rushed to install it. If a GitHub repository started trending, I bookmarked it. I collected tutorials like they were Pokémon cards.
Then something strange happened.
I looked back at the automation scripts I had written over the past year and realized almost none of them depended on the newest technologies. Instead, they relied on Python features that had been sitting in the language for years — features I had either ignored or misunderstood.
That was a humbling realization.
The biggest improvements in my productivity didn’t come from learning another AI framework. They came from finally understanding Python itself.
If you’re building automation tools, AI applications, or developer utilities, these are the eight Python features that completely changed how I write code.
